summary:
Fixed the lost-updates bug.

Problem  
incrementCounter() performed a naïve read-modify-write:
1. GET counter
2. +1
3. SET counter

Under heavy concurrency two requests could read the same value and overwrite each other, so some increments were lost and test.sh failed.

Fix  
Use Redis’ atomic INCR command so the increment is executed completely inside Redis.

app/counter.js (new core)  
• replaced the read-modify-write logic with:  
  const newValue = await client.incr(COUNTER_KEY);

All other behaviour (get/reset) unchanged.

Result  
$ ./test.sh  
Expected: 100  
Got:      100  
PASS: Counter is correct
